title: XQ Platform series
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The '''XQ Platform series''' were a group of quick construction [[Space station/Legends|space installations]] produced by multiple companies including [[Bengel Shipbuilders]], [[Telgorn Corporation/Legends|Telgorn Corporation]], and [[Xizor Transport Systems/Legends|Xizor Transport Systems]].

==Description==
[[File:ZaarinVader.jpg|thumb|left|175px|From the bridge of an XQ2 Platform, Admiral [[Demetrius Zaarin|Zaarin]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] watch a demonstration of the new [[TIE/ad starfighter|TIE Avenger]].]]
The XQ series was armed with six [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], two at the corners of each docking platform. There were also three twin [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turrets, either mounted on the top of each of the hangars, or on the central hub of the space station if that happened to overshadow the hangars. Newer platforms (XQ3 to XQ6) often mounted an additional three turrets, for a total of six, on the underside of the hub as this is a common blind spot in older designs.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref>

The central hub of the Platform contained communication antennae and the [[Emperor's Throne Room/Legends|conning tower or bridge]].

Occasionally, high-priority platforms, such as the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] [[Maarek Stele#Arms race|research facility]] in the [[Parmel system/Legends|Parmel system]], had a [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launcher positioned at the tip of its underside. While it was often used to discourage starfighter attacks, its most important role was to intercept incoming warheads before they could harm the station. Attacking fighters often had to use several concussion missiles of their own to overwhelm this anti-warhead defense.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" />

The XQ series hangars could accommodate up to three squadrons of starfighters, plus transports, shuttles, and other auxiliary craft.

==Usage==
Most XQ series platforms served as outposts and supply depots for both sides in the Galactic Civil War, such as [[DS 5]] where defecting Imperial Admiral [[Harkov]] took refuge. [[Outpost D-34]] and [[NL-1]] served as Imperial customs stations, tasked with guarding major hyperspace lanes and bringing order to the sector.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" />

Others were research installations, like Admiral [[Demetrius Zaarin|Zaarin's]] [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] [[Maarek Stele#Arms race|facility]] in the [[Parmel system/Legends|Parmel system]], and [[Pondut]] and [[RS-32]] which housed [[Mag-pulse warhead/Legends|magnetic pulse]] warhead technology used by the Rebellion.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" />

==Series==
*[[XQ1 Platform]]
*[[XQ2 Platform]]
*[[XQ3 Platform]]
*[[XQ4 Platform]]
*[[XQ5 Platform]]
*[[XQ6 Platform]]

==Behind the scenes==
The XQ Platforms were first introduced in [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']],<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" /> replacing the combinations of freighters and cargo containers used to represent [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] in the preceding [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']].

The naming nomenclature has changed through the X-wing series; for instance in [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], the station were referred to as "Platform Class 1 (PLT/1)", while the following ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]'' it was called "XQ1 Platform". ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' added further information on the manufacturers of the Platforms.

In [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], the concourse is a large circular atrium with a central column (perhaps containing the turbolifts),<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" /> which could imply that it is an XQ series platform rather than a Star Destroyer. If so, this is the only depicted interior of an XQ platform.

The [[XQ2 Platform|XQ2]] appears to be most popular platform. In [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], platforms very similar to the XQ2 (and to a lesser extent, the XQ5) are seen in several cutscenes, such as the introduction (representing a Rebel outpost), Tour of Duty 3 (the new [[NL-1]] in the [[Pakuuni system/Legends|Pakuuni system]]) and Tour of Duty 4 (the test facility for [[TIE/ad starfighter|TIE Avengers]]).<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" /> The cover art of ''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' likely depicts an XQ2 as well. There are several differences between the XQ2 and the platforms depicted in cutscenes/artwork, however, most notable is their undersides where the XQ2's is flat while the latter is semi-spherical.
[[File:Avenger_demo.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Zaarin's TIE Avenger demonstration for Lord Vader at a Platform, likely an XQ2.]]

In [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] Tour of Duty 3, there is an inconsistency between Missions 5 and 6, the [[NL-1]] platform changes from a XQ3 to an XQ1. (Similarly in Tour of Duty 1, there is a blooper as ''[[Fogger]]'' is first represented by a [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B escort frigate]] and then a [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]], in missions 3 and 4, respectively.)

===Size===
There is some discrepancy in the size of the XQ Platform series. In ''TIE Fighter'', an XQ station had a diameter of around one third of a kilometer, with each docking arm being appropriate in size for a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] or several [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]]/[[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]. At the end of Tour of Duty 3, an XQ2 platform is shown as much smaller than an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] standing guard alongside.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" /> Graphically updated with textures for ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]'', the XQ models remained the same size.

However, with the release of ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', the XQ stations had been rescaled to three times the size, now having a diameter of around a kilometer. Each docking arm was now able to comfortably accommodate several spacecraft as large as a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]]. The cover art for ''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' also gives a similar comparison of the XQ Platform (perhaps an [[XQ2 Platform|XQ2]]) to [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]], TIEs, and a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]].
